title: PepsiCo, Inc. (PEP) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of PepsiCo (PEP) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: 5.5% represents steady mid-single-digit expansion. The $8.23B trailing FCF establishes a solid base. Through continuous "productivity initiatives" (automation) and pricing leverage, PepsiCo can realistically grow free cash flow slightly above long-term inflation targets without massive capital expenditures, as they rely on brand equity and entrenched shelf space.

risks.introText: Despite being a foundational portfolio holding, my DCF assumes a low risk premium. Any disruption to PepsiCo's core business lines could rapidly alter the margin of safety:

items.title: 1. GLP-1 and Consumer Health Trends
items.description: The widespread adoption of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs poses a structural threat to Frito-Lay and sugary beverage volumes. If overall caloric intake drops significantly or consumer preferences rapidly pivot to healthier options, revenue growth will stall.

items.title: 2. Pricing Elasticity Breaking Point
items.description: PepsiCo has successfully relied on "shrinkflation" and direct price hikes to protect margins during inflationary periods. The primary risk is reaching a tipping point where consumers trade down to private-label brands, destroying the volume/mix equation.

items.title: 3. Debt Load Sensitivity
items.description: Carrying over $53B in total debt acts as a massive anchor on equity value. In a prolonged "higher for longer" interest rate environment, refinancing this debt will increase interest expense and directly compress Free Cash Flow available to shareholders.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).
